ai_summary:
## Executive Summary: Critical Security & Architecture Issues

**🚨 IMMEDIATE ACTION REQUIRED**

Your Next.js API routes have **severe security vulnerabilities** that expose your application to attacks:

**Critical Security Issues (HIGH Priority):**
- **Multiple unprotected API endpoints** allowing unauthorized mutations - implement authentication middleware immediately
- **No input validation** on request bodies - vulnerable to injection attacks and malformed data
- **Cryptographically insecure `Math.random()`** used for security-sensitive operations - replace with `crypto.randomBytes()` or `crypto.randomUUID()`

**Database Reliability Issues (MEDIUM Priority):**
- **Unhandled Supabase query failures** across multiple endpoints - wrap all database operations in try-catch blocks and return proper error responses
- Missing error handling will cause crashes and poor user experience

**Production Readiness (LOW Priority):**
- **15 console.log statements** left in production code - remove or replace with proper logging

**Recommended Action Plan:**
1. **Week 1**: Add authentication checks to all mutating routes
2. **Week 1**: Replace Math.random() with secure alternatives  
3. **Week 2**: Implement input validation using Zod or similar
4. **Week 2**: Add comprehensive error handling for database operations
5. **Week 3**: Clean up console logs and implement proper logging

**Risk Level: CRITICAL** - Deploy fixes before production release.

findings.title: Mutating API route without authentication check
findings.description: POST/PUT/DELETE routes without authentication allow anyone to modify data.
findings.suggested_fix: Add authentication check at the start of the handler. Verify session or API key before processing the request.

findings.title: API route reading request body without validation
findings.description: Accepting request body without validation allows malformed or malicious data to reach your business logic.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate with Zod: const data = schema.parse(await req.json()). Return 400 for invalid input.

findings.title: Mutating API route without authentication check
findings.description: POST/PUT/DELETE routes without authentication allow anyone to modify data.
findings.suggested_fix: Add authentication check at the start of the handler. Verify session or API key before processing the request.

findings.title: Math.random() used for security-sensitive value
findings.description: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and can be predicted.
findings.suggested_fix: Use crypto.randomBytes() in Node.js or crypto.getRandomValues() in browsers.

findings.title: useEffect missing dependency array
findings.description: useEffect without a dependency array runs after every render, causing infinite loops if state is updated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a dependency array: useEffect(() => {...}, [dep1, dep2]). Use [] for mount-only effects.

findings.title: useEffect missing dependency array

findings.title: Supabase query without error handling
findings.description: Supabase operations return {data, error}. Ignoring the error means failures are silently swallowed.
findings.suggested_fix: Always destructure and check: const {data, error} = await supabase.from(...); if (error) throw error;

findings.title: Array index used as key prop
findings.description: Using array index as key causes incorrect component reuse when items are reordered, added, or removed.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a stable unique identifier: key={item.id}

findings.title: console.log/warn/error left in production code
findings.description: Console statements left in production code leak implementation details and impact performance.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove debug logs before committing. Use a proper logger with log levels for production logging.
